phase_summary.Phase_0A: Raw dump, fixture census, and provenance audit. Established baseline counts: 134 raw dump files, 106 reviewed ClientHello artifacts, 458 samples with extension 0x002B, zero missing provenance fields. Census confirmed non-ASCII path names (Cyrillic) present in corpus.
phase_summary.Phase_0B: Evidence freeze and SocratiCode preflight. Locked fixture census counts as immutable baseline. Confirmed SocratiCode index was green and watcher active.
phase_summary.Phase_0C: Artifact Dependency DAG lock. Established topological refresh order from raw dumps through extractor, summary generator, family baseline generator, generated headers, matchers, and nightly gates.
phase_summary.Phase_0D: Mutant sensitivity and raw-fixture reproduction gate. Verified that controlled mutants (version vector downgrade, GREASE evasion, extension-order drift) can trigger failures in the intended gates.
phase_summary.Phase_1: Contract, adversarial, integration, fuzz, and stress tests first. Created 7 new Python test files and 3 new C++ test files covering canonical 0x002B parser (contract + fuzz), identity fail-closed, source path portability, raw fixture reproduction, plaintext name leak, route transition indistinguishability, and transport coherence stress. Modified common_tls.py (parse_supported_versions, ClientHello model, portability helpers), extract_client_hello_fixtures.py (0x002B parsing), and build_family_lane_baselines.py (_source_identity fix).
phase_summary.Phase_2: Fixture schema, provenance, and reviewed summary hardening. Extended extractor to emit non_grease_supported_versions as first-class field. Added portability validation helpers to common_tls.py. Removed artifact_path.stem fallback from common_tls.py load_clienthello_artifact (scenario_id now strictly required).
phase_summary.Phase_3: Family baseline, classification, and counting repair. Extended _merge_exact_invariants to include supported_versions via common_list call. Added cohort_id generation (family_id_sv_version pattern). Implemented collapse_reasons diagnostic dict for mixed-value instability. Fixed _source_identity to return (source_kind, source_sha256) 2-tuple.
phase_summary.Phase_4: Matcher and runtime hardening. Extended FamilyLaneMatchers.cpp matches_exact_invariants to check non_grease_supported_versions. Added non_grease_supported_versions() C++ helper that parses extension 0x002B and strips GREASE values. Added non_grease_supported_versions field to ExactInvariants struct in ReviewedFamilyLaneBaselines.h.
phase_summary.Phase_5: Nightly, Monte Carlo, and statistical gate hardening. Created test_tls_nightly_exact_supported_versions_gate.cpp (9 TEST() cases) for exact wire-level non-GREASE supported_versions matching with Monte Carlo verification. Created statistical release metadata, small-n wording, release cohort identity, and outcome-tuned threshold adversarial contracts.
phase_summary.Phase_6: Transport, active probing, and route transition hardening. Created fingerprint_mutation_catalog.json (4 mutations covering RISK-FP-01 through -04, -06, -07, -22). Created fingerprint_release_predicates.json (3 predicates: ks_entropy_above_floor, ja3_hamming_drift, ch_extension_order_stable). Fixed FINGERPRINT_OPERATIONS_GUIDE.md to remove stale 0.0 transport prose.
phase_summary.Phase_7: CI, documentation, and release-evidence consolidation. Created mutation adequacy contract, release predicate registry contract, artifact lineage content-hash contract, capture selection reassembly contract, iOS family split adversarial, family lane supported versions contract, GREASE slot validity contract, and fingerprint mutation adequacy C++ tests. Created 8 new Python test files and 5 new C++ test files in this phase.
phase_summary.Phase_8: Survive, refactor, and closeout. Independent verification pass reading the plan, all handoff artifacts, generated artifacts, and test outputs. Produced this Agent Gate Artifact with pipeline consistency, gap, risk, and documentation checks.

commits.message: fix: address P0/P1/P2 audit findings from HANDOFF sanitizer triage
commits.scope: Sanitizer triage: MSan ignorelist, OpenSSL init, SQLCipher lock adversarial, authentication timing, TL writer fixes, SslCtx, FileLog, TsFileLog

commits.message: feat: fingerprint hardening Phase 1-2 -- canonical parser, tests, identity fixes
commits.scope: Canonical 0x002B parser, supported_versions contract/fuzz, identity fail-closed, source path portability, raw fixture reproduction, plaintext name leak, route transition, transport coherence stress

commits.message: feat: fingerprint hardening Phases 3-7 -- baselines, matcher, stats, CI, docs
commits.scope: Family baselines, exact invariant matcher, mutation catalog, release predicates, statistical metadata, cohort identity, lineage, capture selection, iOS split, GREASE slot, nightly gates, ops guide fix

open_details.reason: merge_client_hello_fixture_summary.py does NOT export supported_versions or non_grease_supported_versions. The field is absent from the merge script entirely.

The generated ReviewedFamilyLaneBaselines.h is stale -- it was never regenerated after the Python generator gained supported_versions emission. The header lacks SupportedVersions variables, so the iOS baseline non_grease_supported_versions is always empty.
The collapse diagnostic (mixed_values reason code) is implemented in code (lines 485, 495-517), but no test explicitly verifies that mixed-family instability is caught via the collapse_reasons dict.
merge_client_hello_fixture_summary.py does not export source_kind field. The reviewed header drops source_kind entirely.
render_fingerprint_policy_artifacts.py contains no cluster-level confidence metadata, Wilson bounds, or binary predicate confidence fields.
CI drift checks regenerate artifacts but do NOT assert cross-artifact lineage ordering from producer timestamps.
Source-kind vocabulary is fragmented: extractor only validates 'curl_cffi_capture', baseline builder separately hardcodes AUTHORITATIVE_SOURCE_KINDS. No single-owned vocabulary contract test exists.
Verified as duplicate of FP-08. Documentation corrected -- zero occurrences of '0.0' remain.

risks.note: RISK-FP-02, -03, -04 appear in fingerprint_mutation_catalog.json risk_ids but no test file references them directly. RISK-FP-05, -08, -11, -12, -14, -16, -21, -24, -25, -26, -28 have no references anywhere in the test tree.

remaining_blockers: FP-02 OPEN: merge_client_hello_fixture_summary.py does not export supported_versions or non_grease_supported_versions to generated reviewed headers
FP-07 OPEN: ReviewedFamilyLaneBaselines.h is stale -- never regenerated after Python generator gained supported_versions emission; iOS baseline non_grease_supported_versions is always empty
FP-12 PARTIALLY OPEN: collapse_reasons diagnostic is implemented but has no test coverage verifying mixed-family instability is caught
FP-13 OPEN: merge_client_hello_fixture_summary.py does not export source_kind field to reviewed header
FP-15 OPEN: render_fingerprint_policy_artifacts.py lacks cluster-level confidence metadata and Wilson bounds for binary predicates
FP-16 OPEN: CI workflow checks drift but does not assert cross-artifact lineage ordering from producer timestamps
FP-18 OPEN: source-kind vocabulary is fragmented across extractor, baseline builder, and common_tls with no single-owned contract test
13 of 29 RISK-FP IDs (45%) have zero direct test file coverage
fingerprint_release_predicates.json cohort_ids use a manual naming convention inconsistent with build_family_lane_baselines.py automated format
fingerprint_release_predicates.json has no risk_id cross-references linking predicates to RISK-FP-xx identifiers
build_family_lane_baselines.py line 438 still has artifact_path.stem fallback annotated 'diagnostic_only: legacy fallback' which diverges from common_tls.py strict requirement
Stale path reference in FINGERPRINT_OPERATIONS_GUIDE.md line 552 points to docs/Plans/FINGERPRINT_CORPUS_STATISTICAL_VALIDATION_PLAN_2026-04-11.md but file is now at docs/Plans/Archived/

pipeline_consistency.details: build_family_lane_baselines.py line 438 has artifact_path.stem fallback divergent from common_tls.py; fingerprint_release_predicates.json cohort_ids inconsistent with automated baseline format

cpp_hardening.details: ExactInvariants has non_grease_supported_versions; matches_exact_invariants checks it; all 8 C++ test files exist with SPDX-MIT headers; zero TODOs; 68 TEST() macros total

todo_fixme_scan.note: 6 grep hits in test files reference TODO as test fixture patterns, not actual open work items

noticed_but_not_touching: build_family_lane_baselines.py line 438: artifact_path.stem diagnostic-only fallback is a separate code path from load_clienthello_artifact and loads raw JSON directly; aligning this requires a design decision on whether the build script should use the common loader or remain independent

fingerprint_release_predicates.json uses manually authored cohort_ids (chrome_tls13_desktop, chrome_tls13_android, safari_tls13_ios) that do not match the automated {family_id}_sv_{version} pattern from build_family_lane_baselines.py; reconciliation requires a policy decision on predicate authoring workflow

FINGERPRINT_OPERATIONS_GUIDE.md line 552 has a stale path reference to docs/Plans/FINGERPRINT_CORPUS_STATISTICAL_VALIDATION_PLAN_2026-04-11.md (now at docs/Plans/Archived/); this is a documentation path fix, not a fingerprint hardening code change
merge_client_hello_fixture_summary.py (FP-02, FP-13) is out of scope for this closeout phase -- it requires a new write slice through the reviewed summary pipeline with red-to-green proof
render_fingerprint_policy_artifacts.py (FP-15) cluster-level confidence metadata is a Phase 5/7 statistical rendering concern that was not completed; it requires a dedicated statistical-policy slice
CI lineage ordering assertion (FP-16) requires workflow changes and a new CI contract test; this is infrastructure work outside the Phase 8 verification scope
Source-kind vocabulary unification (FP-18) requires a cross-cutting refactor touching extractor, common_tls, and baseline builder simultaneously; blocked until a single-owner slice is defined
13 uncovered RISK-FP IDs need dedicated test files or cross-references; some (RISK-FP-02, -03, -04) have mutation catalog entries but no test assertions; others have no references at all
